_meta.produced_by: Claude (Anthropic) — MK Series AI layer build from DRS papers

paper_title: The Fractal Rhythm Model: Closed-Loop Governance, Regime-Aware Adaptation, and the Axiom 5 Modification for Dynamic Environments
paper_type_note: Primary contribution is the application of FRM regime detection to Meta-Kaizen governance. Axiom 5-prime is a modification, not a new derivation. Classified application_C because the paper applies FRM to an existing framework.

phase_ready.note: PH-MK4.1: Empirical validation of RDS and CSS against real-world regime shifts pending. PH-MK4.2: Parameter defaults (w_N=0.20, w_I=0.10, delta=0.30) are modelling choices, not derived.

falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-8 — Fracttalix P2 Build Plan S48

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Operational default. Not derived — chosen as balance between sensitivity and false alarm rate. Organizations should calibrate empirically.
Operational default. Ensures zero-novelty candidates receive at most 0.20 effective novelty under maximum regime signal. Not derived from first principles.
Operational default. More conservative than w_N because impact floors are riskier than novelty floors.
Operational default. delta_min formula in Section 6.1 provides principled lower bound.
Operational default. Substrate-specific calibration guidance in Section 4.1.
Derived from extinguishing recursion r_{t+1} = r_t * (1 - delta * a_t). Solving for delta that guarantees r_t < epsilon within H_plan cycles.

claim_registry.label: Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection (BOCP)
claim_registry.name: Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection (BOCP)
claim_registry.statement: Adams & MacKay (2007) BOCP algorithm computes P(change point at t | y_1,...,y_t) via recursive Bayesian updating with geometric run-length prior. Provides exact posterior over run lengths in O(t) time per observation.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 5 — Essentialness with Veto Power (from MK-P1)
claim_registry.name: Axiom 5 — Essentialness with Veto Power (from MK-P1)
claim_registry.statement: A zero value on any single component renders the candidate inadoptable: KVS(0,I',C',T) = KVS(N,0,C',T) = 0. This is the axiom being deliberately modified by Axiom 5-prime.

claim_registry.label: Regime Discontinuity Score (RDS)
claim_registry.name: Regime Discontinuity Score (RDS)
claim_registry.statement: RDS_t = P(change point at t | y_1,...,y_t) computed via BOCP algorithm with geometric run-length prior, hazard rate h = 1/L. Default L = 12 months. RDS_t in [0,1]. Threshold: RDS_t > 0.70 triggers FRM adjustment.

claim_registry.label: Complexity Surge Score (CSS)
claim_registry.name: Complexity Surge Score (CSS)
claim_registry.statement: CSS_t measures volatility-normalized complexity expansion. Two formulations provided: (1) clipping formulation — CSS_t = min(1, (C_t - mu_C) / sigma_C); (2) logistic formulation — CSS_t = 1/(1 + exp(-(C_t - mu_C)/sigma_C)). Both satisfy CSS_t in [0,1].

claim_registry.label: Axiom 5-prime — Regime-Conditioned Essentialness
claim_registry.name: Axiom 5-prime — Regime-Conditioned Essentialness
claim_registry.statement: When a regime signal is active (S_t = max(RDS_t, CSS_t) > 0), candidates with N_j = 0 receive an effective novelty floor w_N * S_t > 0. Bound: w_N * S_t <= 0.20. Restoration: S_t = 0 or w_N = 0 exactly recovers standard KVS (Axiom 5).

claim_registry.label: KVS-hat Formula
claim_registry.name: KVS-hat Formula
claim_registry.statement: KVS-hat_j = max(w_N * S_t, N_j) * max(w_I * S_t, I'_j) * C'_j * T_j. Default weights: w_N = 0.20, w_I = 0.10. When S_t = 0, KVS-hat_j = KVS_j (standard).

claim_registry.label: Extinguishing Recursion
claim_registry.name: Extinguishing Recursion
claim_registry.statement: r_{t+1} = r_t * (1 - delta * a_t), where r_t is residual regime intensity, a_t in {0,1} is whether an improvement was adopted, and delta in (0,1] is the extinguishing rate. Passive waiting does not resolve a regime shift.

claim_registry.label: Axiom 5-prime Restoration Property
claim_registry.name: Axiom 5-prime Restoration Property
claim_registry.statement: Setting S_t = 0 (no regime signal) or w_N = 0 (no novelty floor) in KVS-hat exactly recovers standard KVS. The departure from Axiom 5 is bounded: max effective novelty floor = w_N * 1.0 = 0.20. A standard candidate with N = 0.91 always dominates a zero-novelty candidate.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-3: Substitution — set S_t = 0 in KVS-hat formula.
inference_rule_steps.output: max(0, N_j) * max(0, I'_j) * C'_j * T_j = N_j * I'_j * C'_j * T_j = KVS_j

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — max novelty floor = w_N * max(S_t) = 0.20 * 1.0 = 0.20.
inference_rule_steps.output: 0.20 < 0.91 (standard novel candidate), so novel candidates always dominate.

WHERE.S_t_zero_recovery_fails: boolean · true if KVS-hat_j with S_t=0 differs from KVS_j for any candidate j
WHERE.floor_exceeds_bound: boolean · true if w_N * S_t > 0.20 for any S_t in [0,1] with w_N = 0.20

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: (1) Compute KVS-hat with S_t=0 for 100 random candidates; compare to KVS; (2) compute w_N * S_t for S_t in {0.0, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0}; check all <= 0.20; finite
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Restoration is a design requirement, not an empirical claim. If the formula is implemented correctly, restoration holds by construction.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Implementation bug: max(w_N * S_t, N_j) coded as w_N * S_t + N_j → KVS-hat != KVS when S_t = 0 and N_j > 0} → S_t_zero_recovery_fails = true → FALSIFIED.

claim_registry.label: Extinguishing Recursion Convergence (delta_min)
claim_registry.name: Extinguishing Recursion Convergence (delta_min)
claim_registry.statement: For target planning horizon H_plan and deactivation threshold epsilon, delta_min = 1 - (epsilon/r_0)^(1/H_plan). With H_plan = 8 quarters, epsilon = 0.10, r_0 = 1.0: delta_min = 1 - 0.10^(1/8) = 0.25. Any delta >= delta_min guarantees r_t < epsilon within H_plan cycles.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — assume a_t = 1 every cycle (best case): r_t = r_0 * (1-delta)^t.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — solve r_0 * (1-delta)^H_plan = epsilon for delta.
IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — substitute H_plan=8, epsilon=0.10, r_0=1.0.

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Compute 0.10^(1/8) numerically; subtract from 1; compare to 0.25; also verify: simulate r_t = (1-0.25)^t for t=1..8 and check r_8 < 0.10; finite
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Pure algebraic claim. delta_min formula assumes a_t = 1 every cycle (continuous adoption) — best-case scenario. If adoption is intermittent, convergence takes longer.

claim_registry.label: KVS-hat IPS Worked Example
claim_registry.name: KVS-hat IPS Worked Example
claim_registry.statement: Under simulated market regime shift (correlations 0.22 → 0.61): archived candidate with N=0 receives effective N = 0.20 * 0.92 = 0.184 in Q4. KVS-hat = 0.184 * 0.67 * 0.60 * 1.00 = 0.074. Below kappa = 0.50 but visible. After domain refresh to N=0.91, standard KVS = 0.91 * 0.73 * 0.60 * 1.00 = 0.399.

inference_rule_steps.operation: IR-5: Algebraic manipulation — substitute N=0, S_t=0.92, w_N=0.20 into KVS-hat.

falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Rounding to 2 decimal places: 0.07 → NOT FALSIFIED. Paper reports 0.072 due to intermediate rounding.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Structural demonstration only. The simulated regime shift (correlations 0.22 → 0.61) is an illustration, not empirical data. Empirical validation of RDS and CSS pending (PH-MK4.1).

claim_registry.label: Closed-loop feedback architecture completeness
claim_registry.name: Closed-loop feedback architecture completeness
claim_registry.statement: The FRM operates as a closed-loop feedback system with six components: (1) substrate outcome monitoring, (2) regime signal computation, (3) KVS adjustment, (4) candidate evaluation, (5) principal approval, (6) outcome logging feeding back to Bayesian calibration and regime signal computation. The loop is closed: component 6 feeds back to components 1 and 2.

WHERE.feedback_loop_broken: boolean · true if any of the six components lacks a defined input or output connection to the next component in the loop, OR if component 6 output does not feed back to components 1 and 2

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Trace the data flow through all six components; verify each has defined input and output; verify component 6 outputs are inputs to components 1 and 2; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: A component with optional bypass (e.g., principal approval may be delegated) → NOT FALSIFIED (bypass is a parameterization, not a broken link).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Closed-loop architecture is a design specification. Completeness means all six components are defined and connected — not that an implementation exists.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {Component 6 (outcome logging) does not specify how outcomes feed back to regime signal computation (component 2)} → feedback_loop_broken = true → FALSIFIED.

cross_references.relation: Axiom 5-prime is a deliberate, bounded modification of MK-P1 Axiom 5.
KVS-hat extends KVS formula with regime-conditioned floors.
Closed-loop component 6 feeds back to Bayesian calibration (MK-P2 Section 6).
FRM as regime-detection instrument — primary cross-track bridge.
Extinguishing rate delta connects to MK-P5 intervention timing — when to act on regime signal.
